angle: Vision / category-building (Kirstin Hunter, Denholm-report policy framing)
hook_type: Cadence numbers + 'a clear view of where capital goes next' curiosity, into Kirstin verbatim
tactic: Proof cadence -> category-vision curiosity -> Kirstin Denholm quote -> the platform is raising -> waitlist
primary_text:
324 raises, $234M+, and a clear view of where Australian capital goes next.

"When retail investors, not just wholesale investors and VCs, can back the next generation of Australian deeptech, medtech, and cleantech companies we get a richer, more resilient innovation ecosystem." Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal.

We built the rails for that. Now we are opening our own Crowd-Sourced Funding raise.

The waitlist is free. Read the offer document before you decide.

Leave your email and we will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: Where Australian capital goes next
description: The category vision, in the CEO's words. Save your spot.
_source_label: DRC V5 -- W7 Vision / Category-building (existing investors)
_iterates: New vision/category angle the client + JB asked for; uses QUOTE-009 (Kirstin, Denholm report).
_v5_rationale: Uses QUOTE-009 ad_safe excerpt verbatim, attributed 'Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal'. Policy/category-vision framing for the sophisticated Segment B reader. 'ecosystem' is a banned AI-tell but is inside the attributed verbatim (exempt, same precedent as QUOTE-005 'seamless'); not used in surrounding prose.
Hook Halbert 5/5 in --primary-text mode (cadence + category-vision curiosity gap). 'a clear view of where Australian capital goes next' is the curiosity bound; the Kirstin quote pays it off.
Category vision is opinion/vision per the quotes register: licenses NO market-share / #1 / largest / first claim. Body states none. The only numbers are the verified spine (324, $234M+).

V5b: stronger email-capture CTA (FarmThru-proven pattern). Closing CTA changed from the weaker 'save your spot' framing to an explicit email-capture micro-action ('Leave your email...'), per clients/farm-thru/learnings-meta-ad.md verbatim ('Outcome-stated CTA: leave your email, we will tell you the moment it goes live'). Notification-framed, no transaction verb, AU English, no contractions, CSF-compliant. Risk-reversal sentence, hook, headline, proof cadence, fact citations, and the verbatim CSF warning all unchanged. Button cta field 'Save my spot' (approved WAITLIST-CTA verbatim) unchanged.
_drc_audience: B2C considered, most-aware. Existing Birchal investors who think in terms of the capital-markets category, not just one raise.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'we will tell you the moment', 'read'. No transaction verbs as a CTA. 'back the next generation' is inside Kirstin's attributed verbatim, not a reader instruction.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'. No 'we will send'.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact final line, appears exactly once.

QUOTE-009: Kirstin Hunter ad_safe excerpt verbatim (en-dashes converted to commas, contiguous span), attributed 'Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal'. Banned AI-tell 'ecosystem' exempt inside the attributed verbatim only.
Vision/policy framing licenses no numeric claim: zero market-share / #1 / largest / first / valuation / $1B. Only verified spine numbers used.
Subject discipline: 'Birchal' appears once, inside the attributed quote attribution ('Kirstin Hunter, CEO, Birchal'); body otherwise first-person we/our; no platform-vs-company swap.
